candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A line above operating profit, but it is not labelled as cost of sales / materials / production-related and does not clearly sit outside the selected COGS bucket in a way that can be confidently assigned to COGS.
Separate amortisation line above operating profit, but no indication it relates to COGS rather than broader operating overhead.
Separate operating D&A-type line, but functional allocation to COGS is unclear and could span admin/selling/other functions.
Contra-D&A adjustment line, but not clearly attributable to COGS and including it risks misallocation.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Materials and services'. While separate D&A rows exist above operating profit, none is explicitly tied to cost of sales / cost of revenues. To avoid double counting or functional misallocation, no COGS-specific D&A row is added.

candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line at the same level as the selected SG&A component rows, so it appears outside those selected parent buckets rather than embedded within them. However, the label occurs twice, making exact row identification ambiguous.
Separate amortisation line above operating profit and outside the selected SG&A component rows; appears to be operating D&A that would need separate addition if assigned to overhead.

Separate operating depreciation line above operating profit and outside the selected SG&A component rows; likely part of operating overhead rather than embedded in employee benefits / other opex / sales & marketing / admin rows.
Appears to be a contra-depreciation operating adjustment at the same level as other D&A rows; if D&A is added separately to SG&A, this line may also belong to that same separate bucket.

variables.notes: MANUALLY CHANGED. The selected XSGA bucket is a sum of component rows and does not include any D&A-labelled row. The visible D&A lines appear as separate same-level operating expenses above operating profit, so they should be added separately rather than assumed embedded in the selected SG&A components. Manual review is needed because 'Depreciation' appears twice with the same exact label, creating row-identity ambiguity in the excerpt and making it unclear whether one or both duplicated lines are intended.
